description:
Workflow is the Schema for the Workflow API.
A Workflow orchestrates one or more Tasks with boot options, hardware references, and templating for provisioning Hardware.

apiVersion.description:
APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.
Servers should convert recognized schemas to the latest internal value, and
may reject unrecognized values.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#resources

kind.description:
Kind is a string value representing the REST resource this object represents.
Servers may infer this from the endpoint the client submits requests to.
Cannot be updated.
In CamelCase.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds

spec.description: WorkflowSpec defines Tasks and associated options.

globals.description: Globals are extra configuration that is applied to all Tasks in the Workflow.

envVars.description: EnvVars defined here are additive to any existing environment variables.

items.description: EnvVar defines a single environment variable.

key.description: Key is the name of the environment variable.

value.description: Value is the value of the environment variable.

templateMap.description: TemplateMap is a mapping of key/values that will be used when templating a Task.

volumes.description: Volumes defined here are additive to any existing volumes.

items.description:
Volume is a specification for mounting a location on a Host into an Action container.
Volumes take the form {SRC-VOLUME-NAME | SRC-HOST-DIR}:TGT-CONTAINER-DIR:OPTIONS.
When specifying a VOLUME-NAME that does not exist it will be created for you.
Examples:

Read-only bind mount bound to /data

	/etc/data:/data:ro

Writable volume name bound to /data

	shared_volume:/data

See https://docs.docker.com/storage/volumes/ for additional details.

tasks.description: Tasks that are run as part of the Workflow.

items.description: WorkflowTask defines a Task entry within a Workflow, binding a Task definition to an Agent and Hardware.

agentID.description: AgentID is the ID of the Agent that is to run this Task.

extra.description: Extra configuration that is applied to this specific Task.

envVars.description: EnvVars defined here are additive to any existing environment variables.

items.description: EnvVar defines a single environment variable.

key.description: Key is the name of the environment variable.

value.description: Value is the value of the environment variable.

templateMap.description: TemplateMap is a mapping of key/values that will be used when templating a Task.

volumes.description: Volumes defined here are additive to any existing volumes.

items.description:
Volume is a specification for mounting a location on a Host into an Action container.
Volumes take the form {SRC-VOLUME-NAME | SRC-HOST-DIR}:TGT-CONTAINER-DIR:OPTIONS.
When specifying a VOLUME-NAME that does not exist it will be created for you.
Examples:

Read-only bind mount bound to /data

	/etc/data:/data:ro

Writable volume name bound to /data

	shared_volume:/data

See https://docs.docker.com/storage/volumes/ for additional details.

hardware.description: Hardware is the Hardware and options associated with this Task.

bmcRef.description:
BMCRef is the bmc.tinkerbell.org object associated with this Task.
When specified, the BootOptions will be applied using this bmc.tinkerbell.org object.
Mutually exclusive with HardwareRef.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: name and namespace must both be specified or both be empty

bootOptions.description:
BootOptions are options that control the booting of Hardware.
These are only applicable when a HardwareRef or a BMCRef is provided.

bootMode.description: BootMode is the type of booting that will be done. One of "netboot", "isoboot", or "customboot".

customboot.description:
Customboot is the configuration for the "customboot" boot mode.
This allows users to define custom BMC Operations for pre and post a Task.

postOperations.description:
PostOperations are the BMC operations that will be run after all Task Actions have completed.
In most cases these operations should get a machine into a state where it can be powered off or rebooted and remove any mounted virtual media.
These operations will be run only if all the associated Task Actions complete successfully.

items.description:
Operations defines the operations that can be performed against a BMC.
Only a single field in the struct should be defined at any given time
as only one operation can be performed at a time.
For example either PowerAction or BootDevice.

bootDevice.description: BootDevice is the device to set as the first boot device on the Machine.

device.description: Device is the name of the device to set as the first boot device.

efiBoot.description: EFIBoot indicates whether the boot device should be set to efiboot mode.

persistent.description: Persistent indicates whether the boot device should be set persistently as the first boot device.

powerAction.description: PowerAction represents a baseboard management power operation.

timeoutSeconds.description:
TimeoutSeconds defines the maximum time in seconds the Operation is allowed to run.
If not defined, the default from the runtime is used.

virtualMediaAction.description: VirtualMediaAction represents a baseboard management virtual media insert/eject.

kind.description: Kind represents the kind of virtual media.

mediaURL.description: mediaURL represents the URL of the image to be inserted into the virtual media, or empty to eject media.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: only one of powerAction, bootDevice, or virtualMediaAction can be specified

preOperations.description:
PreOperations are the BMC operations that will be run before any Task Actions.
In most cases these operations should get a machine into a state where a Tink Agent is running.

items.description:
Operations defines the operations that can be performed against a BMC.
Only a single field in the struct should be defined at any given time
as only one operation can be performed at a time.
For example either PowerAction or BootDevice.

bootDevice.description: BootDevice is the device to set as the first boot device on the Machine.

device.description: Device is the name of the device to set as the first boot device.

efiBoot.description: EFIBoot indicates whether the boot device should be set to efiboot mode.

persistent.description: Persistent indicates whether the boot device should be set persistently as the first boot device.

powerAction.description: PowerAction represents a baseboard management power operation.

timeoutSeconds.description:
TimeoutSeconds defines the maximum time in seconds the Operation is allowed to run.
If not defined, the default from the runtime is used.

virtualMediaAction.description: VirtualMediaAction represents a baseboard management virtual media insert/eject.

kind.description: Kind represents the kind of virtual media.

mediaURL.description: mediaURL represents the URL of the image to be inserted into the virtual media, or empty to eject media.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: only one of powerAction, bootDevice, or virtualMediaAction can be specified

isoURL.description:
ISOURL is the URL of the ISO that will be one-time booted.
When this field is set, a job.bmc.tinkerbell.org object will be created
for getting the associated Hardware into a CDROM booting state.
A Workflow Hardware Reference that contains a spec.reference.builtin.bmc must be provided.
BootMode must be set to "isoboot".

toggleNetboot.description:
ToggleNetboot indicates whether the the field in the associated Hardware for allowing network booting should
be enabled before the Task is executed and disabled after the Task has completed successfully.
A Hardware Reference must be provided.

hardwareRef.description:
HardwareRef is the Hardware object associated with this Task.
This is used if the Task has templating that requires Hardware information.
When specified, the BootOptions will be applied using the BMCRef defined in the Hardware object.
Mutually exclusive with BMCRef.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: name and namespace must both be specified or both be empty
hardwareRef and bmcRef are mutually exclusive
at least one of hardwareRef or bmcRef must be specified

taskRef.description: TaskRef is the Task associated with this Workflow.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: name and namespace must both be specified or both be empty

timeoutSeconds.description:
TimeoutSeconds is the duration before a Task time out is reached.
A zero or nil value means no timeout.

TimeoutSeconds is the duration before a Workflow time out is reached.
A zero or nil value means no timeout.

status.description: WorkflowStatus defines the observed state of a Workflow.

bootOptions.description: BootOptions holds the state of any boot options.

allowNetboot.description: AllowNetboot holds the state of the controller's interactions with the netboot disabled field in a Hardware object.

jobs.description: Jobs holds the state of any job.bmc.tinkerbell.org objects created.

additionalProperties.description: JobStatus holds the state of a specific job.bmc.tinkerbell.org object created.

complete.description: Complete indicates whether the created job.bmc.tinkerbell.org has reported its conditions as complete.

existingJobDeleted.description:
ExistingJobDeleted indicates whether any existing job.bmc.tinkerbell.org was deleted.
The name of each job.bmc.tinkerbell.org object created by the controller is the same, so only one can exist at a time.
Using the same name was chosen so that there is only ever 1 job.bmc.tinkerbell.org per Hardware/Machine.bmc.tinkerbell.org.
This makes clean up easier and we dont just orphan jobs every time.

uid.description:
UID is the UID of the job.bmc.tinkerbell.org object associated with this workflow.
This is used to uniquely identify the job.bmc.tinkerbell.org object, as
all objects for a specific Hardware/Machine.bmc.tinkerbell.org are created with the same name.

conditions.description: Conditions are the latest available observations of an object's current state.

items.description: WorkflowCondition describes the current state of a Workflow condition.

message.description: Message is a human readable message indicating details about last transition.

reason.description: Reason is a (brief) reason for the condition's last transition.

status.description: Status of the condition, one of True, False, Unknown.

time.description: Time when the condition was created.

globalExecutionStop.description:
GlobalExecutionStop represents the time when the Workflow should stop executing.
After this time, if the Workflow has not completed it will be marked as timed out.

globalTimeout.description: GlobalTimeout represents the max execution duration time.

metadata.description:
Metadata tracks where the Workflow is in its execution.
It holds the current state of the Workflow as a whole,
the current Task, and the current Action.

action.description: Metadata tracks the state and timing of a single execution unit (Workflow, Task, or Action).

id.description:
UID is a type that holds unique ID values, including UUIDs.  Because we
don't ONLY use UUIDs, this is an alias to string.  Being a type captures
intent and helps make sure that UIDs and names do not get conflated.

task.description: Metadata tracks the state and timing of a single execution unit (Workflow, Task, or Action).

id.description:
UID is a type that holds unique ID values, including UUIDs.  Because we
don't ONLY use UUIDs, this is an alias to string.  Being a type captures
intent and helps make sure that UIDs and names do not get conflated.

workflow.description: Metadata tracks the state and timing of a single execution unit (Workflow, Task, or Action).

id.description:
UID is a type that holds unique ID values, including UUIDs.  Because we
don't ONLY use UUIDs, this is an alias to string.  Being a type captures
intent and helps make sure that UIDs and names do not get conflated.

renderedTasks.description: RenderedTasks are the Tasks to be run by this Workflow.

items.description:
TaskWithMetadata is a copy of TaskSpec with the addition of the ActionWithMetadata.
Users don't need Metadata in the CRD Spec so we don't include it but we need
it in the CRD Status.

actionMetadata.description: Metadata tracks the state and timing of a single execution unit (Workflow, Task, or Action).

id.description:
UID is a type that holds unique ID values, including UUIDs.  Because we
don't ONLY use UUIDs, this is an alias to string.  Being a type captures
intent and helps make sure that UIDs and names do not get conflated.

args.description: Args are a set of arguments to be passed to the command executed by the container on launch.

background.description:
Background or Detach indicates that the Action should be run in the background and not block the execution of other Actions.
The Action will report back as successful before the Action is executed in the background.
Then the Action will be run in the background and move on to the next Action immediately without waiting for completion.
This is useful for Actions that do things like kexec, reboot, or power off a machine.

command.description:
Command defines the command to use when launching the image. It overrides the default command
of the Action image. It must be a unix path to an executable program. When omitted, the image's
default command/entrypoint is used.

envVars.description: EnvVars defines environment variables that will be available inside a container.

items.description: EnvVar defines a single environment variable.

key.description: Key is the name of the environment variable.

value.description: Value is the value of the environment variable.

image.description:
Image is an OCI image. Should generally be a fully qualified OCI image reference.
For example, quay.io/tinkerbell/actions/image2disk:v1.0.0 or docker.io/library/alpine:3.23

name.description: Name is a human readable name for the Action.

namespaces.description: Namespaces defines the Linux namespaces with which the container should configured.

retries.description: Retries is the number of times the Action should be run until completed successfully.

timeoutSeconds.description:
TimeoutSeconds is the total number of seconds the Action is allowed to run without completing
before marking it as timed out.

volumes.description: Volumes defines the volumes that will be mounted into the container.

items.description:
Volume is a specification for mounting a location on a Host into an Action container.
Volumes take the form {SRC-VOLUME-NAME | SRC-HOST-DIR}:TGT-CONTAINER-DIR:OPTIONS.
When specifying a VOLUME-NAME that does not exist it will be created for you.
Examples:

Read-only bind mount bound to /data

	/etc/data:/data:ro

Writable volume name bound to /data

	shared_volume:/data

See https://docs.docker.com/storage/volumes/ for additional details.

environment.description: Environment variables here are added to all Actions in the Task.

key.description: Key is the name of the environment variable.

value.description: Value is the value of the environment variable.

name.description: Name is a human readable name for the Task.

don't ONLY use UUIDs, this is an alias to string.  Being a type captures
intent and helps make sure that UIDs and names do not get conflated.
